source_key: 15.1 A polynomial is a constructed transformation
title: A polynomial is a constructed transformation
role: Make a polynomial feel assembled from shifts, scalings, and powers.
prompt_core: Illustrate a machine assembled from modules labeled only by their visual action: a power-raising chamber, a scaling lever, a shift block, and an addition junction, all combining to generate one smooth output curve. The image should communicate that a polynomial is built from operations we already understand. Editorial-machine style, adult and precise.
caption: A polynomial is a recipe built from familiar transformations.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:
Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "15.1 A polynomial is a constructed transformation".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Make a polynomial feel assembled from shifts, scalings, and powers.

Layout intent:
H — Half-page conceptual plate

Style direction:
assembled-machine plate

Core image brief:
Illustrate a machine assembled from modules labeled only by their visual action: a power-raising chamber, a scaling lever, a shift block, and an addition junction, all combining to generate one smooth output curve. The image should communicate that a polynomial is built from operations we already understand. Editorial-machine style, adult and precise.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
A polynomial is a recipe built from familiar transformations.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

source_key: 15.2 Roots are where the machine outputs zero
title: Roots are where the machine outputs zero
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Turn roots into crossings where the transformation hits the horizontal axis.
prompt_core: Show a polynomial curve crossing the x-axis at several points, with each crossing read as a place where the machine’s output vanishes. The axis should feel like a meaningful threshold, not just graph paper decoration. Elegant analytic illustration, optional overlay for curve specifics and factor hints.
caption: A root is where the transformation lands exactly on zero.
overlay: Overlay exact curves later if needed.

source_key: 15.3 The dimension insight: abstract polynomials are usually ratio-machines
title: The dimension insight: abstract polynomials are usually ratio-machines
role: Prevent dimensional nonsense by showing why raw lengths cannot usually be plugged into abstract polynomials unchanged.
prompt_core: Create a split image where one side tries to add incompatible geometric species—a length term, an area term, a pure number term—and visibly fails to cohere, while the other side shows a clean dimensionless polynomial machine working on pure ratios. The image should feel like a powerful correction, not scolding. Transparent layers and type discipline are key.
caption: Polynomials usually live in the world of pure ratios unless coefficients carry the missing dimensions.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

title: Why polynomials matter so much
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
role: Show polynomials as the simplest smooth curves that approximate richer behavior.
prompt_core: Design a bridge image in which simple polynomial curves act like scaffolding for more complicated smooth behaviors, gradually approximating richer functions. The composition should suggest that polynomials are the training ground and local language of calculus. Clean, elegant, future-leaning, with a sense of increasingly expressive smoothness.
caption: Polynomials matter because they are the first smooth language rich enough to approximate much more.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. source_key: 15.5 Historical note: from Babylon to the Renaissance and beyond
title: Historical note: from Babylon to the Renaissance and beyond
role: Give polynomial problem-solving a long civilizational arc.
prompt_core: Create a restrained historical montage from ancient equation-solving on clay or parchment through Renaissance algebraic ambition into modern curve-based understanding. Avoid turning it into a jumble of names; focus on the enduring desire to solve structured symbolic problems and see their geometric consequences. Warm history meeting modern clarity.
caption: Polynomial thinking has been part of the subject’s backbone for millennia.

title: Thought experiments
role: Invite the learner to imagine turning transformation knobs and watching axis crossings move.
prompt_core: Show a simple curve with a few conceptual knobs for shifting up, stretching, or changing curvature, and watch its zero crossings slide. The sketch should feel like exploratory play for a serious mind, not gamification. Minimal, lucid, graph-like.
caption: Change the machine slightly and watch where its output hits zero.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles.
